name: Autologous mitochondrial transplant for acute cerebral ischemia phase 1
summary: A phase 1 acute ischemic stroke study testing endovascular autologous mitochondrial transplantation during standard mechanical thrombectomy, with matched controls used for safety comparison.
population: Adults with acute ischemic stroke undergoing mechanical thrombectomy; treated subjects ranged from 43 to 80 years old

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Mitochondrial Delivery and Transplantation because they can show whether the intervention changes safety, function, and biomarkers in Adults with acute ischemic stroke undergoing mechanical thrombectomy; treated subjects ranged from 43 to 80 years old. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
